mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with t-stat > 2.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) during 2005-2006 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise during post-shock recovery periods, with mean forward returns indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL breaching 195.0, with forward returns exceeding +1.5% in > 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates sustainable aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, resulting in a mean forward return below zero during synchronized expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative equity returns during late-cycle expansions, with forward returns remaining positive in > 50% of cases.
